full_prompt: Design the passenger-cell carbon-fiber monocoque for a 2025 Bridgestone World Solar Challenge Cruiser Class vehicle, compliant with international solar-car benchmark 2025 Regulations v1.0. Vehicle gross mass with 2 occupants and battery 500 kg. Solar array area <= 6 m^2. Battery energy <= 11 MJ (3.056 kWh). Monocoque is a carbon/epoxy prepreg sandwich laminate with Nomex honeycomb core; outer skin [45 / 0 / -45 / 90]s laid up as 0.25 mm plies, core thickness 20 mm, inner skin [45 / 0 / -45 / 90]s. Material: carbon/epoxy T700S/epoxy (E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, strength L = 1500 MPa, T = 50 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3); Nomex honeycomb core density 48 kg/m^3, out-of-plane compressive strength 2.4 MPa, out-of-plane shear strength 1.2 MPa. Monocoque bounding 2500 mm (length) x 1600 mm (width) x 1100 mm (height). Integral 2-point anchor pads for roll bar stubs, 4 seat belt anchorages per seat. Crashworthiness / international solar-car benchmark 2025 required load cases: (R1) Rollover: vehicle shall support 1.0 x vehicle mass x g = 1.0 x 500 x 9.81 = 4,905 N applied at the top of the roll bar stubs (the monocoque transfers this into the A-pillar/roof structure); max laminate Tsai-Wu index <= 1.0 and core shear stress <= 1.2 MPa. (R2) Side impact: 3 g lateral applied at seat belt anchor heights on both seats, 3 x 500 x 9.81 = 14,715 N total distributed 50/50 to the two seats; max Tsai-Wu index <= 1.0. (R3) Frontal: 10 g forward applied to the full 500 kg at the front-bulkhead plane, 49,050 N; max Tsai-Wu <= 1.0; peak deceleration at occupant centerline <= 25 g (not a monocoque stress check but a crash simulation output). (R4) Occupant-belt loads per FMVSS 210 analog: 13.5 kN pulled on upper lap belt and 13.5 kN pulled on shoulder belt simultaneously at each seat; max Tsai-Wu at anchor interface <= 1.0. (R5) Monocoque mass <= 50 kg. (R6) First modal frequency hard-mounted at 4 chassis mount points >= 30 Hz. Primary verification: laminate-composite structural FEA (linear static for R1, R2, R4; explicit dynamics recommended for R3). Secondary: modal for R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 outer mold-line + laminate schedule, and a report listing monocoque mass in kg, max Tsai-Wu per load case, core shear stress in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design the passenger-cell carbon/epoxy sandwich monocoque for a 2025 Bridgestone World Solar Challenge Cruiser Class vehicle, compliant with the international solar-car benchmark 2025 Regulations v1.0. Monocoque carries 2 occupants + battery (gross 500 kg); solar array area <= 6 m^2; battery energy <= 11 MJ (3.056 kWh). Outer and inner skins are T700S carbon/epoxy quasi-isotropic laminates around a Nomex honeycomb core; integral 2-point anchor pads for roll-bar stubs and 4 seatbelt anchorages per seat.

Envelope: bounding 2500 x 1600 x 1100 mm (length x width x height).

Load cases.
- LC1 rollover: 1.0 x vehicle mass x g = 4,905 N at the top of the roll-bar stubs (fed into the A-pillar/roof); core shear check.
- LC2 side impact: 3 g lateral x 500 kg = 14,715 N total split 50/50 between the two seatbelt anchor heights.
- LC3 frontal: 10 g forward x 500 kg = 49,050 N at the front-bulkhead plane; peak decel at occupant centerline <= 25 g.
- LC4 belt loads (FMVSS 210 analog): 13.5 kN on the upper lap belt and 13.5 kN on the shoulder belt simultaneously at each seat.
- LC5 modal: hard-mounted at 4 chassis mount points, first frequency >= 30 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Frontal 10 g crash sim for occupant-decel (R3) is an explicit dynamics problem.

prompt.title: international solar-car benchmark 2025 Cruiser Class carbon monocoque
prompt.brief: CFRP/Nomex sandwich passenger cell, 500 kg GVM. Passes rollover, side, frontal, and belt-anchor load cases.

boundary_conditions.loads: point or distributed at belt anchors, roll bar stubs

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Sandwich panel skins evaluated via Tsai-Wu.

eval_coverage: CFRP sandwich monocoque under four structural load cases
